zh.Cromarty, Ross & Cromarty, Scotland: 蘇格蘭可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪
zh.lifeSummary: 當Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray於大約1097年出生在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾時，他的父親Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray48歲，母親Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland40歲。他於1135年之前與Mariotte MacGillebride在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1168年10月23日在蘇格蘭可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪享年72歲。

zh-hans.East Lothian, Scotland: 英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安
zh-hans.Cromarty, Ross & Cromarty, Scotland: 苏格兰可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray于大约1097年出生在苏格兰艾爾金爾时，他的父亲Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray48岁，母亲Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland40岁。他于1135年之前与Mariotte MacGillebride在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1168年10月23日在苏格兰可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: mac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Cuong, Minh, Lien, Muoi, Thanh, Tho, Tieu, Vinh, Anh, Beo, Binh, Chau.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 麥 (see   Mai   2) and 莫 (see   Mo   1).
Vietnamese (Mạc): from Chinese 莫 ‘nothing; nonexistent’; see Mo 1.

variants.name: Mai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Hoa, Son, Dung, Long, Binh, Minh, Thu, Hue, Huong, Thuy.  Chinese Chi, Hong, Ho, Sang, Han, Hang, Jian, Li, Man, Mei, Sen, Wei.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 麥, meaning ‘wheat’ in Chinese: (i) from Mai Qiu (麥丘), the title of a man in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), who, despite being very old, came to celebrate the birthday of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). His descendants inherited 麥, the first part of his title, as their surname. (ii) borne by descendants of Mai Tie Zhang (麥鐵杖), a famous general during the Sui dynasty (581–618  AD ). His descendants were scattered around southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 買, meaning ‘buy’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of the king Song Weizi, founder of the state of Song. (ii) borne by descendants of the Duke Dao of Xu (ruler of the state of Xu, 546–523  BC ), possibly inheriting his personal name. (iii) from the first element of the personal name Mai Zhu Chu (買朱鉏), style name of an official who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) it has also been traced back to other ethnic groups in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Máil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  Scottish:  see  Meil  .   Black  , at Meal, notes that the surname may not have originated in Shetland but derive from the settlement of some Orkney families there in the early 17th century.
2  Welsh, English:  see  Male  .
3  Irish:  see  Malley  .

variants.name: Malley
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Malley  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in northern France called Mailly (Marne, Somme, and elsewhere).
Americanized form of Rusyn (from Slovakia) Mlej (see Malay ).

variants.name: Male
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Mael (literally ‘prince’).

English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French mail ‘mallet, club, mace’, or Middle English (Old French) maille ‘ring (of chain mail), armour’, or Old French maille ‘speckle, stain’, or Middle English maille (Old French maaille) ‘coin worth half a penny’, or from a shortened form of Anglo-Norman French amel, Old French esma(i)l ‘enamel’ (compare Mailer ). Any of these terms might have been used to characterize an individual's behaviour, occupation, or appearance.

English: nickname Middle English (Old French) male ‘bag, pouch’, also ‘stomach, belly’, perhaps given to someone who wore a distinctive pouch or bag or who made such bags (compare the Middle English occupational term malemakere), or to someone with a prominent belly.

variants.name: Black
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Meil
meanings: Scottish (Orkney): habitational name from one of several places in Orkney (and perhaps Shetland) named from Old Norse melr ‘sandbank’. The surname is now spelled Meil in Orkney and Mail in Shetland. The name is rare in Britain and Ireland.
German: variant of   Meile  .

nameMeanings.name: Máel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Fanciful spelling of Gail , also occasionally written Gaelle .

variants.name: Gail
meanings: Shortened form of   Abigail .  It was not found as an independent given name before the middle of the 20th century; it became popular in the 1950s and 1960s, but has since fallen out of fashion.

nameMeanings.name: Coluim
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Colm .

variants.name: Colm
meanings: Traditional Irish form of the Late Latin personal name Columba ‘dove’. See Callum .

en.lifeSummary: When Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray was born about 1097, in Elginshire, Scotland, his father, Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray, was 48 and his mother, Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland, was 40. He married Mariotte MacGillebride before 1135, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 23 October 1168, in Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray est né environ 1097, à Elginshire, Écosse, son père, Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Mariotte MacGillebride avant 1135, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 23 octobre 1168, à Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray um 1097 in Elginshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray 48 und seine Mutter Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland 40. Er hat vor 1135 in Scotland Mariotte MacGillebride geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Oktober 1168 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray è nato il circa 1097 nel Elginshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland, aveva 40 anni. Lui sposato Mariotte MacGillebride prima del 1135, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 23 ottobre 1168, nel Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland, all'età di 72 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Morayが約1097年にスコットランドエルジンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Morayは48歳，母Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotlandは40歳であった。1135年以前にスコットランドでMariotte MacGillebrideと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1168年10月23日にスコットランドクロマルトイシュアイアクロマルトイで72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray(이)가 대략 1097년 스컷랜드 엘긴실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1135년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Mariotte MacGillebride(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1168년 10월 23일 스컷랜드 크로마티실르 크로마티에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray nasceu aproximadamente 1097, em Elginshire, Scotland, seu pai, Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Mariotte MacGillebride antes 1135, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de outubro de 1168, em Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray родился около 1097 г. в местности элгиншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Mariotte MacGillebride перед 1135 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 23 октября 1168 г. в местности кромерти, кромертишир, Шотландия в возрасте 72 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Máel Coluim mac Máil Brigti, Mormaer of Moray nació aproximadamente 1097, en Elginshire, Scotland, su papá, Aed Heth I Mormaer Of Moray, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Mariotte MacGillebride antes de 1135, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 23 de octubre de 1168, en Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland, a la edad de 72 años.
